differential_diagnoses: Monoclonal gammopathy of renal significance (MGRS) with proliferative glomerulonephritis
Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Nephrotic syndrome (secondary to MGRS or amyloid)
next_steps: Perform renal biopsy with immunofluorescence and electron microscopy
Obtain quantitative urine protein (24-hour urine collection or spot protein/creatinine ratio)
Perform echocardiography with global longitudinal strain imaging to evaluate for amyloid cardiomyopathy
Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics
Consider abdominal fat pad aspiration for Congo red staining
Optimize decongestion therapy with careful monitoring of blood pressure and renal function
